blocks.description: Allowing all origins means any website on the internet can make authenticated requests to your API, potentially stealing user data.
blocks.code:
from flask import Flask
from flask_cors import CORS

app = Flask(__name__)
# DANGEROUS: any website can make requests to your API
CORS(app, origins="*", supports_credentials=True)

blocks.description: Whitelist only the domains you control. Use environment variables so staging and production have different allowed origins.
blocks.code:
import os
from flask import Flask
from flask_cors import CORS

app = Flask(__name__)

allowed_origins = os.environ.get(
    "CORS_ORIGINS", "http://localhost:3000"
).split(",")

CORS(
    app,
    origins=allowed_origins,
    supports_credentials=True,
    methods=["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"],
    allow_headers=["Content-Type", "Authorization"],
)
